title: English Teachers
description:
English Teacher (Preschool / Childcare) - 3 positions

Job Purpose:

To plan and deliver engaging English language lessons that support children’s holistic development, in accordance with ECDA curriculum frameworks and the centre’s philosophy, while ensuring a safe, nurturing, and stimulating learning environment.

Key Responsibilities:

Teaching & Learning

  • Plan, prepare, and implement age-appropriate English language lessons aligned with ECDA’s Nurturing Early Learners (NEL) Framework.
  • Foster children’s development in listening, speaking, reading, and early writing skills through play-based and inquiry-based learning.
  • Create a positive, inclusive, and engaging classroom environment that supports children’s social and emotional development.
  • Observe, assess, and document children’s progress, and provide feedback to parents during conferences and through learning portfolios.

Classroom Management & Care:

  • Ensure children’s safety, well-being, and hygiene at all times.
  • Establish clear routines, expectations, and positive behaviour guidance strategies.
  • Maintain classroom cleanliness, organisation, and appropriate learning displays.


Curriculum & Programme Support:

  • Participate in curriculum planning, centre events, excursions, and enrichment activities.
  • Collaborate with co-teachers and teaching assistants to support children with diverse learning needs.
  • Contribute to the preparation of lesson plans, learning resources, and documentation as required by the centre and ECDA.

Parent & Stakeholder Engagement:

  • Build positive relationships with parents through regular communication and updates.
  • Address parents’ queries professionally and work collaboratively to support each child’s development.

Professional Responsibilities:

  • Comply with all ECDA regulations, centre policies, and SOPs.
  • Attend staff meetings, training, and professional development programmes.
  • Uphold confidentiality, professionalism, and ethical standards at all times.

Job Requirements:

Qualifications:

  • Minimum **Diploma in Early Childhood Care & Education (DECCE-T)** or equivalent ECDA-recognised qualification.
  • Certified by **ECDA** (L1/L2 Teacher category as applicable).

Experience:

  • At least 3 years of teaching experience in a preschool or childcare setting preferred.
  • Fresh graduates with strong practicum experience may be considered.

Skills & Competencies:

  • Strong command of spoken and written English.
  • Passion for working with young children.
  • Patient, nurturing, and positive disposition.
  • Good classroom management and organisational skills.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Additional Information:

  • Working hours: As per centre operations
  • Location: Woodland, Sims and Central area
  • Employment type: Full-time

postedCompany.description:
Philosophy:

At Cherie Hearts, we believe that children are unique individuals who deserve to reach their potential. Our purposeful multi-literacies curriculum approach enhances children's holistic growth and allow children to experience personal success and develop a joy for learning.

Vision:

We partner our parents by listening to what they want for their children. We aim to be an established preschool that promote lifelong learning and equips children with competencies.

Mission:

We seek to nurture and educate children with a comprehensive foundation in early childhood development.

People Excellence:

We believe in advancing our educators who are our most valuable resources by fostering their talent through professional development, training and support. Our children are lovingly nutured by competent, dedicated teachers who show:

Positive attitude, Competency, Committment and

Creativity
postedCompany.name: BUCKET HOUSE PRESCHOOL@SIMS AVENUE PTE. LTD.
